claim: v0.2 remains inspectable but is not executable under v0.3 trust semantics

findings.exploit: cached validation fields could promote edited records
findings.correction: recompute every coordinate from raw CAS bytes

findings.exploit: a record could carry an attacker key
findings.correction: pin one Ed25519 key per policy principal

findings.exploit: legacy Booleans could imply external compatibility
findings.correction: remove the legacy Boolean authority path

findings.exploit: projection bytes could differ from the raw JSON pointer
findings.correction: reconstruct exact pointers and compare RFC 8785 digests

findings.exploit: unknown, expired, revoked, or scoped keys could enable state
findings.correction: bind principal role, source, schema, scope, and lifetime

findings.exploit: multi-file writes could expose partial authority
findings.correction: commit immutable generations through one atomic CURRENT pointer

findings.exploit: portable copies omitted v0.3 generation authority
findings.correction: bundle CURRENT, generations, and CAS and rerun strict doctor

findings.exploit: JSON output could select success despite process failure
findings.correction: make timeout, truncation, schema, and exit status authoritative

findings.exploit: an adapter could target the workspace or change executables
findings.correction: pin executable digest and reject workspace argv paths

findings.exploit: undeclared nested fields could bypass shallow filters
findings.correction: close every native schema with unevaluatedProperties false

findings.exploit: optimistic branches could receive optional progress credit
findings.correction: propagate all four worst-case abstract outcomes

findings.exploit: resource dimensions were absent from dominance
findings.correction: retain unit-separated resource intervals in Pareto comparison

findings.exploit: witness-invented food or balances could establish formation
findings.correction: derive markings from receipt-backed state and require strict prior layers

findings.exploit: closure could be confused with self-maintenance
findings.correction: require closed organization and strictly positive exact flux

findings.exploit: catalysis could be circular or unrelated to targets
findings.correction: bind generalized generative RAF layers to the productive target set

findings.exploit: positive resource cycles could escape incomplete enumeration
findings.correction: use an exact dual open-system resource potential certificate

findings.exploit: bounded siphon search could be reported as absence
findings.correction: return unknown unless exhaustive within declared limits

findings.exploit: empty or partial perturbations could enable robustness
findings.correction: require nonempty suites and replay full fixed-point state

findings.exploit: shared exposure could still count as independent evidence
findings.correction: derive domains from keys, infrastructure, lineage, correlation, and precommit exposure

findings.exploit: first-time commands referenced nonexistent files
findings.correction: ship an installed signed local tutorial generator

findings.exploit: critical branches were untested
findings.correction: enable branch coverage and per-core gates
